figures.gloss: The mysterious peddler who appeared in Detroit in 1930, recognized by the Nation of Islam as the supreme being, Allah, incarnate in human form. He revealed the Supreme Wisdom, sparked the awakening of Black Americans, and vanished without a trace in 1934.
figures.facet: Rejects the incorporeal, spirit-God of orthodox Islam and Christianity. God is historically and physically manifested as the supreme Black man, collapsing theology into a radical racial ontology.
figures.provenance: Supreme Wisdom Lessons; 'Message to the Blackman in America'.

figures.gloss: Born Elijah Poole, he was chosen by W.D. Fard as the sole Messenger to the 'Lost-Found Nation.' For over forty years, he systematized the movement's theology, strict dietary laws, and vision of economic independence.
figures.facet: Displaces the finality of Prophet Muhammad of Arabia. He is the eschatological bridge specifically designated to resurrect the intellectually dead Black man in the wilderness of North America.
figures.provenance: 'Message to the Blackman in America'; 'The Fall of America'.

figures.gloss: A rogue, brilliant Black scientist who, some 6,600 years ago on the island of Patmos, instituted a ruthless eugenics program to graft the white race out of the original Black nation — a race of 'devils' allotted 6,000 years of temporary rule.
figures.facet: Evil is not a fallen angelic spirit but a genetically engineered phenotype. The demonic is stripped of the supernatural and recast as a historical, biological consequence.

figures.gloss: A massive, city-sized spacecraft engineered by original Black scientists, currently orbiting Earth. It contains 1,500 smaller ships armed with powerful explosives, waiting to obliterate the white world system when divine judgment is called.
figures.facet: Fuses Ezekiel's biblical visions with mid-century UFOlogy and technological supremacy. Apocalyptic judgment is executed not by angels with swords, but by vastly superior extraterrestrial Black aeronautics.
figures.provenance: 'The Fall of America'; NOI exegesis of Ezekiel 1:16.

figures.gloss: The uncreated, primordial architects of the universe. In NOI cosmology, the original Black man is 'the Maker, the Owner, the Cream of the Planet Earth, and the God of the Universe,' acting communally through a council of 24 scientists.
figures.facet: Completely reverses global white supremacy by positing Blackness as the literal, eternal divine default of the cosmos. The universe was mathematically engineered by Black men, not spoken by a phantom spirit.

figures.gloss: The elaborate system of deception, historical erasure, and social control engineered by Yakub's white descendants to keep the original Black nation asleep and ignorant of its divine cosmic heritage.
figures.facet: Reframes systemic racism and chattel slavery from mere economic or political oppression into an explicitly satanic, cosmological technology designed specifically to bind the minds of gods.
figures.provenance: Speeches of Malcolm X; NOI sermons.

figures.gloss: The specific ancient lineage of the original Black nation that survived a catastrophic cosmic explosion, which tore the Moon from the Earth, later settling in the Nile Valley and Mecca. Black Americans are their 'lost-found' descendants.
figures.facet: Anchors African American identity in an unimaginably vast, galactic antiquity. It bypasses the trauma of the transatlantic slave trade to claim a cosmological genealogy older than the Earth's current geology.

figures.gloss: The 600-year process of selective breeding mandated by Yakub, where the brown, red, yellow, and finally white races were systematically extracted from the original Black germ, progressively stripping away the divine essence.
figures.facet: An absolute inversion of biological evolution. Here, 'evolution' into lighter skin is a carefully engineered devolution into moral bankruptcy, physical weakness, and sociopathy.

edges.note: Intra-tradition bond: Yacub utilizes eugenics to literally reverse the genetic and spiritual polarity of the Original Man, breeding a physically oppositional race.
Intra-tradition bond: Clarence 13X bridged the NOI concept of God from a specific historical figure (Fard) to the universalized, awakened Black man (A.L.L.A.H.).
Intra-tradition bond: Fard historically absorbed and mutated the esoteric 'Asiatic' identity and Moorish cosmological frameworks established earlier by Noble Drew Ali.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Elijah Muhammad explicitly identified W.D. Fard as the incarnate fulfillment of the biblical Jehovah, the Long-Awaited Messiah.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Fard is theologically identified as the returning Son of Man from the New Testament, collapsing Christian eschatology into his physical arrival in Detroit.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both figures serve as the architect of a corrupted counter-creation; Angra Mainyu creates pests and death, while Yacub grafts the 'devil' race.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Like the Gnostic Demiurge, Yacub is a rogue, localized creator whose artificial genesis traps humanity in a cycle of ignorance and material oppression.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both are highly intelligent but spiritually rebellious actors who isolate themselves to improperly engineer creation, bringing chaos to the original harmony.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both posit an original, uncorrupted divine essence currently trapped or suppressed within a hostile world system, awaiting esoteric realization.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The Original Man is defined as the 'Maker' and 'Owner', possessing an innate, self-generating creative power functionally analogous to the Memphite demiurge.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The Mother Plane is a mechanized reinterpretation of Ezekiel's biblical vision, transforming the divine chariot of God into a literal, apocalyptic spacecraft.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The vessel exists to definitively conclude the 6,000-year cycle of Yacub's grafted world, mechanizing the apocalyptic reset of a cosmic age.
Intra-tradition: The divine sender and his exclusive, irreplaceable earthly messenger.
Intra-tradition: The cosmic adversary and the structural system of deception he birthed to rule the world.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Explicitly adopts the Islamic name and ultimate authority of God, but radically reimagines him as a living Black man.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The impending, apocalyptic purification of the world order through a definitive act of fire and destruction.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: A prophetic figure who synthesizes previous revelations into a highly systematized, dualistic cosmology for a new era.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: A vast, orchestrated illusion designed specifically to keep divine beings trapped in a state of ignorance and slumber.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The perfect, uncreated, and primordial source from which all subsequent lesser reality is derived or devolved.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Primordial, semi-divine ancestors who survive a cosmic transition to seed and populate the earthly realm.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: A specific historical rupture that fundamentally altered the nature of reality, introducing corruption, limitation, and mortality.

Parent seam: Fard emerged from the documented Moorish Science milieu of 1929-30, and the early NOI carries Drew Ali's frame (Asiatic identity, Islam as Black nationality) forward; Moorish claims that Fard was Drew Ali reincarnated remain contested.
The NOI's Tribe of Shabazz reworks Drew Ali's prior teaching of a lost 'Asiatic' nationality for Black Americans — documented conceptual descent within the same movement family.

dossier.essence: A Black nationalist religious movement founded in Detroit in 1930 by the enigmatic W. D. Fard (Wallace Fard Muhammad) and built into a national institution by Elijah Muhammad (born Elijah Poole, leader 1934–1975). It teaches that the Black man is the original and divine human, that Fard was Allah in person, and that Elijah was his Messenger — doctrines sharply divergent from Sunni Islam.
dossier.cosmos: NOI cosmology inverts American racial hierarchy into sacred history: the Original Asiatic Black Man, self-created and eternal (the Tribe of Shabazz), is the maker of civilization, while the white race is a biological product bred some six millennia ago by the rebel scientist Yakub and allotted a fixed span of rule now ending. It classically denies a conventional spirit-afterlife, teaching that heaven and hell are earthly conditions and that 'resurrection' is the mental awakening of Black people from ignorance. Judgment falls instead on the white world-order, to be destroyed from the Mother Plane, a wheel-like craft read out of the vision of Ezekiel.
dossier.practice: The Nation is congregational and disciplined: temples and mosques, the paramilitary Fruit of Islam, strict dietary and dress codes (drawn together in Elijah Muhammad's 'How to Eat to Live'), prohibition of alcohol, tobacco, and sex outside marriage, and a program of Black economic self-reliance and separatism. Members study the catechetical 'Supreme Wisdom' lessons; the movement is famous for prison and street proselytizing and for figures such as Malcolm X and Muhammad Ali. After Elijah's death in 1975 his son Warith Deen Mohammed moved most followers toward Sunni Islam, while Louis Farrakhan rebuilt the original NOI from 1977–78.
dossier.canon: Elijah Muhammad's 'Message to the Blackman in America' (1965) and 'The Fall of America' (1973); the catechetical 'Supreme Wisdom Lessons' attributed to Fard; and the movement newspapers 'Muhammad Speaks' and later 'The Final Call'.
dossier.reflects: Theodicy as racial reversal — the transmutation of slavery and white supremacy into a cosmology in which the oppressed are the original gods and history is a scheduled vindication.
sources: C. Eric Lincoln, 'The Black Muslims in America' (1961)
Edward E. Curtis IV, 'Black Muslim Religion in the Nation of Islam, 1960–1975' (2006)
Karl Evanzz, 'The Messenger: The Rise and Fall of Elijah Muhammad' (1999)
Martha F. Lee, 'The Nation of Islam: An American Millenarian Movement' (1996)
